title: ISE Multi-Site Topology — Cross-Site Replication Lag and WAN Loss Impact

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.5 ICT risk management — cross-site continuity.
A.5.30 ICT readiness for BC — cross-site continuity.
compliance.controlObjective: ICT business-continuity readiness evidence for the cloud ISE deployment.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(c) — BC and crisis management cross-site.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Drop WAN to a lab remote site. ISE replication lag spikes; UC-17.1.61 fires showing the region with WAN flaps + lag.
controlTest.negativeScenario: WAN restored. Both metrics return to baseline.

dataSources: Combines UC-17.1.28's replication-lag feed with WAN-link metrics from `index=network sourcetype=cisco:ios:netflow` or BGP-session events. Per-site / per-region groupings are driven by `ise_deployment.csv` (`node`, `site`, `region`, `wan_circuit`).
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` + Cisco network TA + IPSLA / NetFlow.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" "ReplicationStatus")
  OR (index=network sourcetype="cisco:ios" "%LINEPROTO-5")
| eval region = coalesce(region, "unknown")
| stats max(LastSyncDelta) as max_lag_s, count(eval(like(_raw, "%LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN%"))) as wan_flaps by region
| where max_lag_s > 600 OR wan_flaps > 2
description: Correlates ISE cross-site replication lag with WAN-link instability — distinguishes ISE-internal issues from WAN-induced lag.
value: Multi-site ISE deployments are sensitive to WAN quality. When lag spikes, knowing whether it's ISE-internal or WAN-driven changes the recovery path. This UC provides that distinction.
implementation: Schedule every 15 min, search window `-1h@h to @h`.
visualization: (1) Per-region lag timechart. (2) Per-region WAN-flap timeline. (3) Correlation panel: lag vs flaps.

references.title: Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — Distributed Deployment

mitreAttack: N/A (topology observability — measures network device authentication coverage, no adversary technique)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Multi-site ISE deployment.
• Network TA collecting WAN events.

Step 1 — Configure data collection.
Step 2 — Search.
Step 3 — Validate.
Step 4 — Dashboard combining lag and WAN.
Step 5 — Troubleshooting: prioritise WAN over ISE if both correlate.
grandmaExplanation: ISE has copies in different cities. When the connection between cities is bad, the copies can't keep up with each other. We watch both the ISE-side and the network-side, so we can tell which side is at fault.
